description:
§22.4.1 runtime (b) — `emit.raw(html)` passes its argument to the block splitter VERBATIM (no escape normalization). The source string value holds a literal two-char `
` (backslash-n, from the `\n` source escape); emit.raw does NOT normalize it, so the rendered <pre> textContent is the literal `a
b`. Contrast with the sibling meta-emit-normalize-escape (`emit()`, which normalizes `
` to a real newline → "a b").
rationale: §22.4.1: `emit.raw(html)` SHALL NOT apply any escape-sequence normalization; the string SHALL be passed to the block splitter exactly as provided. A literal backslash-n in the value therefore survives verbatim into the rendered text (it is NOT converted to a newline), so the <pre> textContent is the two-character sequence `\n` between `a` and `b`.
